title: rutaceae
intro: The Rutaceae rank, also known as the citrus family, is a taxonomical rank that includes a diverse group of flowering plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This rank is characterized by its distinctive flowers, often with a strong citrus scent, and its characteristic arrangement of leaves. The Rutaceae family encompasses a wide range of plant species, including citrus fruits such as oranges, lemons, and limes, as well as various species of rue, citrus-root, and other related plants. With over 160 genera and more than 1,500 species, the Rutaceae family is one of the most economically and culturally significant plant families worldwide.
